vulnerabilities.description: Remote Desktop services exposed to network

artifacts.notes: Authorized HR onboarding partner domain identified during SOC investigation

findings.title: Externally identifiable VPN portal
findings.description: A VPN-related domain was identified during reconnaissance.
findings.evidence: Domain vpn.mockorg.local present in lab asset inventory.
findings.recommendation: Review exposure and require MFA for all remote access.

findings.title: Mail portal increases phishing attack surface
findings.description: Mail infrastructure is visible in the environment and may be a target for credential harvesting.
findings.evidence: Mail server and mail portal naming conventions observed in recon data.
findings.recommendation: Harden authentication, user awareness, and mailbox protections.

findings.title: Web application host identified
findings.description: A web application VM was identified as part of the mock target environment.
findings.evidence: Host and IP documented during lab setup.
findings.recommendation: Validate software versions and perform vulnerability review.

findings.title: IOC match detected for domain
findings.description: Artifact mail.mockorg.local matched a simulated IOC.
findings.recommendation: Investigate the asset, validate the indicator, and isolate affected systems if needed.

findings.title: IOC match detected for file_hash
findings.recommendation: Investigate the asset, validate the indicator, and isolate affected systems if needed.

findings.title: High-risk attack path identified: Phishing to Mail Access
findings.description: Attack path 'Phishing to Mail Access' could allow the objective: Obtain user credentials and access internal resources.
findings.recommendation: Reduce exposure by patching vulnerabilities, strengthening authentication, and segmenting access.

findings.title: High-risk attack path identified: Web Exploitation to Internal Compromise
findings.description: Attack path 'Web Exploitation to Internal Compromise' could allow the objective: Exploit vulnerable services for deeper access.
findings.recommendation: Reduce exposure by patching vulnerabilities, strengthening authentication, and segmenting access.

findings.title: Phishing attack simulation demonstrates credential harvesting risk
findings.description: TryHackMe phishing lab demonstrated how attackers can trick users into revealing credentials.
findings.evidence: Observed indicators such as suspicious sender domain and credential capture page.
findings.recommendation: Provide phishing awareness training and implement email filtering controls.

findings.title: Suspicious external email link alert investigated
findings.description: SOC alert triggered by inbound email containing an external link.
findings.evidence: TryHackMe SOC alert investigation showed link belonged to authorized HR partner domain hrconnex.thm.
findings.recommendation: Continue monitoring inbound external links and maintain allow-list for trusted third-party partners.

findings.title: Detection alert generated: Remote Access Portal Detection
findings.description: Artifact vpn.mockorg.local triggered detection rule DR3.
findings.evidence: Flag remote access portals for additional review.
findings.recommendation: Validate the alert, review related activity, and tune detection logic if needed.

findings.title: Detection alert generated: Suspicious Mail Domain
findings.description: Artifact mail.mockorg.local triggered detection rule DR1.
findings.evidence: Detect mail-related domains that may be associated with phishing activity.
findings.recommendation: Validate the alert, review related activity, and tune detection logic if needed.

findings.title: Detection alert generated: Known Malicious Attachment Hash
findings.evidence: Detect file hashes associated with known malicious attachments.
findings.recommendation: Validate the alert, review related activity, and tune detection logic if needed.

findings.title: Multiple remote access services exposed
findings.description: Nmap scan revealed several exposed remote access services including SSH, RDP, and VNC.
findings.evidence: Ports 22, 3389, and 5901 detected open during TryHackMe Nmap lab.
findings.recommendation: Restrict remote access services and require strong authentication mechanisms.

attack_paths.name: Phishing to Mail Access
attack_paths.objective: Obtain user credentials and access internal resources

attack_paths.name: Web Exploitation to Internal Compromise
attack_paths.objective: Exploit vulnerable services for deeper access

alerts.notes: Flag remote access portals for additional review.
Detect mail-related domains that may be associated with phishing activity.
Detect file hashes associated with known malicious attachments.
